headline: VDC Is Up Nearly 6% While the S&P 500 Sinks, and That Gap Is No Accident
summary: When the broader market has lost ground year-to-date and the VIX is hovering near 27, the question investors are quietly asking is: what in my portfolio is actually holding up? Vanguard Consumer Staples ETF (NYSEARCA:VDC) is up nearly 6% year-to-date while the S&P 500 is down about 4% over the same stretch. That gap is ... VDC Is Up Nearly 6% While the S&P 500 Sinks, and That Gap Is No Accident

headline: Wall Street Bulls Look Optimistic About Coca-Cola (KO): Should You Buy?
summary: According to the average brokerage recommendation (ABR), one should invest in Coca-Cola (KO). It is debatable whether this highly sought-after metric is effective because Wall Street analysts' recommendations tend to be overly optimistic. Would it be worth investing in the stock?

headline: Major outgoing CEOs are citing AI as a factor in their decisions to step down
summary: Coca-Cola CEO James Quincey and former Walmart CEO Doug McMillon have told CNBC that the next wave of artificial intelligence is a reason for their departures.

headline: A massive chunk of Berkshire Hathaway’s $300 billion portfolio is invested in just three artificial intelligence stocks
summary: According to recent filings, about 20% of Buffett’s legendary firm is now invested in three Big Tech stocks.

headline: Coca-Cola CEO James Quincey: It was time for someone else to lead the next wave of growth
summary: Coca-Cola Chairman and CEO James Quincey joins 'Squawk Box' to discuss why he's stepping down as company CEO, thoughts on his successor Henrique Braun, his legacy at the company, lessons on driving innovation, what's next in his career, and more.

headline: Should Coca-Cola’s Fairlife Expansion And ESG Scrutiny Require Action From Coca-Cola (KO) Investors?
summary: In March 2026, Coca-Cola disclosed a wave of shareholder proposals on sustainability, plastics, DEI and product-ingredient risks ahead of its April 29 annual meeting, while also announcing long-serving director Maria Elena Lagomasino will retire from the board after 18 years. At the same time, Coca-Cola committed up to US$650,000,000 to expand Fairlife production in Michigan and New York, reinforcing investor attention on how its capital allocation, product mix and sustainability reporting...
